findings.title: Hardcoded secret detected
findings.description: The variable 'SECRET_KEY' appears to contain a hardcoded secret.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secure secret manager.

findings.title: Use of eval() detected
findings.description: The eval() function can execute arbitrary code and may lead to remote code execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsing or explicit logic instead.

findings.title: Unsafe subprocess usage with shell=True
findings.description: Using subprocess with shell=True may allow command injection.
findings.recommendation: Use shell=False and pass command arguments as a list.

findings.title: Weak hashing algorithm detected
findings.description: The code uses hashlib.md5, which is weak for security-sensitive hashing.
findings.recommendation: Use SHA-256, bcrypt, Argon2, or another secure hashing approach.

findings.title: Debug mode enabled
findings.description: Debug mode may expose sensitive stack traces and internal application information.
findings.recommendation: Disable debug mode in production environments.

findings.title: Insecure CORS wildcard detected
findings.description: Using wildcard CORS origins may allow untrusted websites to interact with the API.
findings.recommendation: Restrict CORS origins to trusted frontend domains.

findings.title: Possible raw SQL string formatting
findings.description: SQL queries built using string formatting or concatenation may cause SQL injection.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM query builder.

findings.title: Direct innerHTML assignment detected
findings.description: Assigning content to innerHTML may introduce XSS if the content includes user input.
findings.recommendation: Use textContent or sanitize HTML before assigning it to the DOM.

findings.title: Sensitive token stored in localStorage
findings.description: Storing authentication tokens in localStorage can increase exposure to XSS attacks.
findings.recommendation: Prefer secure, HttpOnly cookies for sensitive session tokens.

findings.title: JavaScript eval() usage detected
findings.description: JavaScript eval() can execute arbitrary code and may lead to code injection.
findings.recommendation: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsing or explicit logic instead.

findings.title: Possible hardcoded secret detected
findings.description: A possible hardcoded secret, password, token, or API key was found in a text-based file.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to environment variables or a dedicated secret manager.

findings.title: Debug configuration enabled
findings.description: Debug mode appears to be enabled in a configuration file.
findings.recommendation: Disable debug mode in production configurations.

findings.title: Wildcard CORS setting in configuration
findings.description: Wildcard CORS configuration may allow untrusted origins to interact with the application.
findings.recommendation: Restrict CORS settings to trusted frontend origins.

findings.title: Docker container runs as root
findings.description: Running containers as root increases the impact of container compromise.
findings.recommendation: Create and use a non-root user inside the Docker image.

dependency_vulnerabilities.description: Unintended leak of Proxy-Authorization header in requests
dependency_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Review GHSA-j8r2-6x86-q33q in OSV.dev and upgrade the affected dependency to a patched or currently supported version.

dependency_vulnerabilities.description: The Requests package before 2.20.0 for Python sends an HTTP Authorization header to an http URI upon receiving a same-hostname https-to-http redirect, which makes it easier for remote attackers to discover credentials by sniffing the network.
dependency_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Review PYSEC-2018-28 in OSV.dev and upgrade the affected dependency to a patched or currently supported version.

dependency_vulnerabilities.description: Requests is a HTTP library. Since Requests 2.3.0, Requests has been leaking Proxy-Authorization headers to destination servers when redirected to an HTTPS endpoint. This is a product of how we use `rebuild_proxies` to reattach the `Proxy-Authorization` header to requests. For HTTP connections sent through the tunnel, the proxy will identify the header in the request itself and remove it prior to forwarding to the destination server. However when sent over HTTPS, the `Proxy-Authorization` header must be sent in the CONNECT request as the proxy has no visibility into the tunneled request. This results in Requests forwarding proxy credentials to the destination server unintentionally, allowing a malicious actor to potentially exfiltrate sensitive information. This issue has been patched in version 2.31.0.
dependency_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Review PYSEC-2023-74 in OSV.dev and upgrade the affected dependency to a patched or currently supported version.

llm_findings.title: Insecure Secret Storage
llm_findings.description: The SECRET_KEY is hardcoded and easily accessible.
llm_findings.recommendation: Store the secret key securely using a secrets management system or environment variables.

llm_findings.title: /danger Endpoint Vulnerable to Code Injection
llm_findings.description: The /danger endpoint uses eval() function which can lead to code injection attacks.
llm_findings.recommendation: Replace eval() with a safe evaluation method or disable the endpoint altogether.

llm_findings.title: /run Endpoint Vulnerable to Command Injection
llm_findings.description: The /run endpoint uses subprocess.run() function which can lead to command injection attacks.
llm_findings.recommendation: Disable the /run endpoint or use a safe method for executing shell commands, such as using os.system() with whitelisted commands.

llm_findings.title: Insecure Token Storage
llm_findings.description: Storing sensitive data like auth tokens in local storage is not secure.
llm_findings.recommendation: Use a more secure method to store and manage authentication tokens, such as using a secure token service or encrypting the data.

llm_findings.title: Arbitrary Code Execution
llm_findings.description: The `runDangerous` function uses eval() to execute arbitrary code, which can lead to code injection attacks and arbitrary code execution.
llm_findings.recommendation: Do not use eval() or any other method that allows executing arbitrary code. Instead, consider using a safer evaluation function or parsing the input in a safe way.

security_gate.reason: Security gate failed because Critical vulnerabilities were detected.
security_gate.policy: FAIL if one or more Critical findings exist.
